samples.gvd:
module pulse_detect
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input data_in,
    output data_out
);
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Design a 1-bit pulse detector.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="data_in">1-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="s_idle">Idle state.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s_1">State 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s_2">State 2.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s_3">State 3.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state">State register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    The module pulse_detect implements a 1-bit pulse detection logic. It takes a clock signal (clk), a reset signal (rst_n), and a 1-bit input signal (data_in) and produces an output signal (data_out).
    The state register (state) is used to keep track of the current state of the pulse detection process. It is updated in a sequential logic block triggered by the positive edge of the clock signal and the negative edge of the reset signal.
    The state machine transitions through four states: s_idle, s_1, s_2, and s_3. In the reset state (s_idle), the state register is set to s_idle, and the output signal (data_out) is set to 0, indicating no pulse.
    In the remaining states (s_1, s_2, s_3), the state machine checks the value of the input signal (data_in) to determine the next state. If the input signal is 0, it transitions to the next state. If the input signal is 1, it transitions back to s_1.
    The output signal (data_out) is set to 1 only in state s_3, indicating the end of a pulse. In all other states, the output signal is set to 0.
    </implementation>
</module>
